pi_items.source: Task setup script and install_wireshark.sh which downloads it from https://wiki.wireshark.org

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on 'dns.cap', a real sample capture from the official Wireshark repository containing 38 DNS packets. All 38 packets have the Recursion Desired bit set (dns.flags.recdesired == 1), and 19 response packets have the Recursion Available bit set (dns.flags.recavail == 1), meaning the server does support recursion. However, there is a critical bug in the setup script (`setup_task.sh`): it uses invalid display filters (`dns.flags.rd == 1` and `dns.flags.ra == 1`), which cause `tshark` to error out and return 0 for both counts. As a result, the ground truth incorrectly expects 0 recursive queries, 0 recursion available responses, and `false` for server recursion support. An agent providing the true correct answers (38, 19, and `true`) will fail the verifier logic.
